title: QUIC CRT internal early-data prerequisite capture work item
summary: Capture the minimum dormant early-data prerequisite material behind the detached resumption carrier while keeping early data explicitly closed and the accepted resumption boundary unchanged.
verification_plan: Run focused requirement-home tests for positive capture, dormant client setup storage, missing early-data-signal absence, closed early-data admission, the public no-0-RTT guard, and the accepted-resumption regression; then run the relevant CRT/API/INT guard set, the full `REQ_QUIC_CRT_` sweep, and SpecTrace render/validation checks.
planned_changes:
- Retain the ticket `max_early_data_size` hint together with the cloned peer transport-parameter snapshot behind the dormant detached resumption carrier.
- Keep the captured early-data prerequisite material internal so no public resumption or early-data promise widens.
- Leave actual 0-RTT packet emission, receive handling, anti-replay, and key-update behavior for later traced slices.
- Add focused requirement-home coverage proving the retained dormant prerequisite state and the still-closed early-data boundary.
completion_notes: The managed client/runtime path now retains the ticket's `max_early_data_size` hint together with the cloned peer transport-parameter snapshot as dormant internal state, but early data stays explicitly closed and no public resumption promise widens.
out_of_scope: opportunistic refactors unrelated to the new early-data prerequisite carrier
